message:
feat(mvcc,pager): add striped occupancy counter to shared lock table and extract WAL commit batch helper

Add StripedOccupancyCounter to the MVCC shared lock table using 16
cache-line-aligned atomic stripes to distribute occupancy increment
contention across separate cache lines during concurrent page lock
insertions. Uses multiplicative hash mixing for stripe selection. The
total count is reconstructed via saturating fold over relaxed loads,
which is correct for the metadata-only read path.

Extract WAL frame collection into a standalone collect_wal_commit_batch
helper in the pager, consolidating the frame ordering, db_size
computation, and commit-flag assignment that was previously inlined in
the commit path. The refactored flow returns an Option<WalCommitBatch>
so the caller only escalates to EXCLUSIVE lock and writes when there
are actual dirty pages, making the no-op commit case free.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1295,7 +1295,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-db300","title":"Epic: many-core write-path reclamation to leapfrog legacy SQLite","description":"## Context
FrankenSQLite is still materially behind legacy SQLite on real Beads databases even after removing several catastrophic self-inflicted costs. The current honest repo-harness baseline remains roughly: c1 disjoint SQLite 7528.8 ops/s vs FrankenSQLite MVCC 1145.6 and forced single-writer 1156.9; c4 mixed_read_write SQLite 2769.5 vs MVCC 424.2 vs single-writer 130.9; c8 mixed_read_write SQLite 965.3 vs MVCC 620.1 vs single-writer 80.8. MVCC already retries less than SQLite under contention, so the remaining gap is primarily per-successful-transaction cost, synchronization, and hot-path waste rather than just conflict rate.

## Program Shape
This epic turns that diagnosis into an explicit many-core performance program for Threadripper/Xeon-class machines. The program is organized around measurement, synchronization removal, batched publish, VDBE and decode reduction, many-core architecture, optional async compile, single-writer cleanup, and correctness or rollout gates. The graph is intentionally self-contained so future execution can proceed from Beads alone.

## Success Criteria
- The graph covers the full optimization program and its verification obligations.
- Dependency edges remain honest and acyclic.
- Every implementation path can be understood from the beads themselves without reopening earlier planning discussions.
- The final scorecard can distinguish real wins from benchmark noise or one-profile lab wins.","design":"- Preserve the core product invariant: concurrent-writer mode stays ON by default. Nothing in this epic may regress FrankenSQLite back into a file-level single-writer design.
- Treat evidence as a first-class artifact. Every major optimization needs comparable before/after numbers on the same real Beads fixtures, workload matrix, retry policy, and build profile.
- Prefer per-core private work, bounded publish, and lock-free or sharded publication over global synchronization. Many-core scale will not come from one giant shared critical section.
- Optimize both MVCC mode and forced single-writer mode honestly. MVCC is the flagship path, but the comparison path must not be pathologically worse because of avoidable overhead.
- Require tests, crash/fault validation, and a final scorecard that distinguishes real wins from benchmark noise.","acceptance_criteria":"- The Beads graph covers measurement, synchronization removal, WAL/pager rewrite, VDBE/decode work, many-core architecture, optional async pre-JIT, single-writer cleanup, and correctness/rollout gates.
- Every implementation bead includes concrete acceptance criteria that mention validation or benchmark evidence, not just code changes.
- Dependency edges reflect real blockers and remain acyclic.
- Future work can infer the motivation, constraints, and target outcomes from the beads themselves without consulting the earlier markdown discussion.
- This bead set explicitly includes comprehensive unit-test, end-to-end, and structured-logging obligations rather than treating verification as implied follow-up work.","notes":"Known findings already established before this epic was created:
- File-backed MemDatabase snapshot/reload work on commit was erroneously happening and has already been removed.
- File-backed reopen was eagerly hydrating rows into MemDatabase even though pager-backed execution uses on-disk pages; that has already been corrected.
- Dirty-page re-sorting on commit was removed.
- Harness fairness bugs around retries and throughput accounting were fixed.
- JIT scaffold bookkeeping on the execute path was pure overhead; making it opt-in by default yielded about a 6.18% speedup on the c8 mixed_read_write MVCC spot check.

Current hotspot evidence motivating the rest of the graph:
- strace showed futex dominating traced syscall time at roughly 79-81% on the hot c8 path.
- perf stat showed low CPU utilization for an 8-way workload, around 0.55-0.58 CPUs utilized, which is a strong sign that synchronization/serialization is suppressing parallelism.
- sampled profiles showed sort, memmove, malloc/free, parser/AST churn, WAL refresh, and per-page append work in the hot set.
- Static analysis of pager/WAL showed the exclusive publish window is too large and still performs per-page allocation/copy/checksum work.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:44:09.945224033Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:23:36.875496849Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","alien-graveyard","architecture","benchmark","db300-program","mechanical-sympathy","mvcc","next-gen-architecture","parallelism","perf","sqlite"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:46:40.705700696Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:47:13.898929628Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:47:48.141271932Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:48:22.830543786Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:48:57.184400339Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:49:33.276223947Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:50:09.277278585Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:50:44.494464617Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2104,"issue_id":"bd-db300","author":"codex","text":"Context snapshot as of 2026-03-10:
- Honest baseline after earlier fixes still leaves FrankenSQLite far behind SQLite on real Beads fixtures.
- MVCC is already retrying less than SQLite under contention, which means the main remaining problem is per-transaction engine cost and synchronization, not simply conflict rate.
- The clearest currently observed waste buckets are futex-heavy waiting, per-page WAL append work under an exclusive window, allocation/copy churn, and decode/materialization overhead.
- The architectural north star for this epic is: parallel prepare/serialize on many cores, tiny serialized publish, lock-free reader publication, and bounded background work that never contaminates latency-sensitive execution.","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:46:01Z"},{"id":2110,"issue_id":"bd-db300","author":"codex","text":"Hierarchy note: this epic uses container->child blocker edges instead of child->parent links so container beads do not block the real work beads. Tracks in this epic are bd-db300.1 Track A, bd-db300.2 Track B, bd-db300.3 Track C, bd-db300.4 Track D, bd-db300.5 Track E, bd-db300.6 Track F, bd-db300.7 Track G, and bd-db300.8 Track H. This remains the authoritative working plan for the post-2026-03-10 real-fixture write-path investigation.","created_at":"2026-03-10T06:24:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.1","title":"Track A: measurement foundation and profiler-safe evidence","description":"This track makes the measurement system trustworthy enough to steer aggressive optimization. The current harness is useful, but pure-engine profiling is still contaminated by post-run integrity checks and benchmark metadata capture, and future work needs a canonical fixture/workload matrix so results remain apples-to-apples across SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer modes.","design":"- Separate pure-engine profiling from end-to-end correctness validation without removing either.
- Freeze a canonical matrix built from the Beads databases already being used in `/dp` and the repo's working benchmark area.
- Standardize artifact naming and hardware-counter capture so future regressions can be compared mechanically.","acceptance_criteria":"- There is a profiler-safe harness mode for hot-path analysis.
- The canonical matrix, retry policy, and artifact naming are explicit.
- Later optimization beads can point to a stable evidence pipeline instead of ad hoc commands.
- This bead set explicitly includes comprehensive unit-test, end-to-end, and structured-logging obligations rather than treating verification as implied follow-up work.","notes":"Relevant current facts: the hot c8 MVCC profile included integrity-check and metadata noise; the real fixtures live under `/dp` and `sample_sqlite_db_files/working/beads_bench_20260310`; the benchmark binary for perf work is `realdb-e2e` built with `--profile release-perf`.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:46:04.260513252Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T17:10:30.679373690Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","harness","measurement","perf","profiling","scorecard","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:51:19.442743596Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:52:04.765225290Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:52:38.316805520Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:53:13.251166920Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:53:48.327530621Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:54:22.931614088Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2105,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1","author":"codex","text":"Evidence snapshot motivating Track A:
- The current perf runs use `realdb-e2e` with `--profile release-perf` and real Beads fixtures under `/dp`.
- Pure engine profiles are currently polluted by post-run integrity checks and benchmark metadata collection such as tool/version capture.
- Future hotspot work needs a clean mode that isolates engine execution while still preserving separate correctness passes.","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:47:53Z"},{"id":2111,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1","author":"codex","text":"Track A flow: A1 profiler-safe harness mode, A2 canonical matrix, A3 automated artifact capture, A5 dominant-bound classification, A6 hardware-topology and placement contract, A4 scorecard thresholds. The point of this track is to make every later optimization reproducible and interpretable on real many-core hardware.","created_at":"2026-03-10T06:24:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.1.1","title":"A1: add profiler-only realdb-e2e mode with optional integrity/metadata suppression","description":"Add a profiler-oriented `realdb-e2e` mode that can skip integrity-check and benchmark-metadata work when the goal is to sample the engine hot path itself. Current perf output is contaminated by post-run validation and tool/version capture, which makes sampled hotspots harder to interpret.","acceptance_criteria":"- The harness exposes an explicit mode or flags for pure-engine profiling.
- Integrity checking and metadata capture remain available as separate validation steps.
- Tests or harness-level checks prove that the new mode does not silently change default correctness behavior.
- The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","notes":"This bead exists because the current c8 MVCC profile still includes work from `integrity_check` and benchmark metadata collection. Future profiling should isolate engine execution from post-run bookkeeping.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IronSpruce","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:51:42.566339566Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T17:10:30.834532424Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","harness","measurement","perf","profiling","track-a"],"comments":[{"id":2109,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.1","author":"IronSpruce","text":"Claimed and in progress. Implementing profiler-only realdb-e2e run flags for integrity-check and environment-metadata suppression while preserving the default correctness path. Reserved crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/realdb_e2e.rs and related report/docs surfaces in Agent Mail.","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:23:18Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-db300.1.2","title":"A2: freeze the canonical Beads fixture and workload matrix","description":"Freeze the canonical benchmark matrix for this optimization campaign using the Beads databases already in `/dp` and the repo's working benchmark area. The matrix must make it impossible to accidentally compare different fixtures, workloads, retry policies, build profiles, placement profiles, or hardware classes when claiming wins.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The matrix explicitly names fixture source paths plus immutable fixture identifiers or digests, workloads, concurrency levels, modes, retry policy, build profile, seed policy, placement profile, and hardware-class identifier.
2. SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer runs are all represented, with baseline and recommended pinned placement profiles always included and an adversarial topology profile included where relevant or available.
3. Output locations and naming conventions are stable enough for mechanical diffing over time and can be linked back to the source revision and Beads data hash used to produce them.
4. The matrix is explicit enough that later scorecard and verification beads can distinguish a transferable win from a lucky one-machine result without reinterpreting raw logs by hand.","notes":"This should cover the real Beads fixtures already in use, including the `frankensqlite`, `frankentui`, and `frankensearch` databases, plus the challenging workloads already proven useful: `commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys`, `hot_page_contention`, and `mixed_read_write`, augmented with the placement profiles needed for honest many-core comparisons.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureCedar","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:51:52.803754130Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T17:49:42.596154659Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","fixtures","measurement","perf","realdb","track-a"],"comments":[{"id":2136,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.2","author":"AzureCedar","text":"Collapsed the duplicate Beads benchmark campaign compile surface back onto crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/fixture_select.rs so there is one canonical contract again. Aligned the checked-in JSON schema and RUNNING_THE_E2E_SUITE.md with the real manifest, added real-manifest schema/artifact-path tests, and kept the bead claimed/in_progress. Verification: rch exec -- cargo check -p fsqlite-e2e --all-targets passed; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_bd_db300_1_2_lib cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e --lib beads_benchmark_campaign -- --nocapture passed (5/5). cargo fmt --check is still red because unrelated dirty files already exist in connection.rs/perf_runner.rs/fixture_select.rs formatting, and targeted clippy still fails in unrelated crates/fsqlite-wal/src/wal_fec.rs:1780 (clippy::needless_pass_by_ref_mut).","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:49:42Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-db300.1.2","title":"A2: freeze the canonical Beads fixture and workload matrix","description":"Freeze the canonical benchmark matrix for this optimization campaign using the Beads databases already in `/dp` and the repo's working benchmark area. The matrix must make it impossible to accidentally compare different fixtures, workloads, retry policies, build profiles, placement profiles, or hardware classes when claiming wins.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The matrix explicitly names fixture source paths plus immutable fixture identifiers or digests, workloads, concurrency levels, modes, retry policy, build profile, seed policy, placement profile, and hardware-class identifier.
2. SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer runs are all represented, with baseline and recommended pinned placement profiles always included and an adversarial topology profile included where relevant or available.
3. Output locations and naming conventions are stable enough for mechanical diffing over time and can be linked back to the source revision and Beads data hash used to produce them.
4. The matrix is explicit enough that later scorecard and verification beads can distinguish a transferable win from a lucky one-machine result without reinterpreting raw logs by hand.","notes":"This should cover the real Beads fixtures already in use, including the `frankensqlite`, `frankentui`, and `frankensearch` databases, plus the challenging workloads already proven useful: `commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys`, `hot_page_contention`, and `mixed_read_write`, augmented with the placement profiles needed for honest many-core comparisons.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureCedar","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:51:52.803754130Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:28:35.116272790Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T18:28:35.115825222Z","close_reason":"Completed: canonical Beads benchmark matrix/schema/artifact contract verified in the live tree via rch-targeted test and cargo-check passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","fixtures","measurement","perf","realdb","track-a"],"comments":[{"id":2136,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.2","author":"AzureCedar","text":"Collapsed the duplicate Beads benchmark campaign compile surface back onto crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/fixture_select.rs so there is one canonical contract again. Aligned the checked-in JSON schema and RUNNING_THE_E2E_SUITE.md with the real manifest, added real-manifest schema/artifact-path tests, and kept the bead claimed/in_progress. Verification: rch exec -- cargo check -p fsqlite-e2e --all-targets passed; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_bd_db300_1_2_lib cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e --lib beads_benchmark_campaign -- --nocapture passed (5/5). cargo fmt --check is still red because unrelated dirty files already exist in connection.rs/perf_runner.rs/fixture_select.rs formatting, and targeted clippy still fails in unrelated crates/fsqlite-wal/src/wal_fec.rs:1780 (clippy::needless_pass_by_ref_mut).","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:49:42Z"},{"id":2137,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.2","author":"BlackDove","text":"Verification pass on the already-landed A2 work:
- The canonical Beads benchmark campaign compile surface is consolidated in `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/fixture_select.rs`, with `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/lib.rs` no longer exporting the stale duplicate module.
- The tracked schema at `sample_sqlite_db_files/manifests/beads_benchmark_campaign.v1.schema.json` matches the checked-in manifest shape, and the real-manifest artifact-path tests are exercised from the `fixture_select` test surface.
- Offloaded verification passed via `rch`:
  1. `rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e beads_benchmark_campaign -- --nocapture` => 5/5 relevant tests passed (`test_expand_beads_benchmark_campaign`, `test_validate_beads_benchmark_campaign_sample`, `test_load_beads_benchmark_campaign_real`, `test_beads_benchmark_campaign_manifest_matches_json_schema`, `test_real_beads_benchmark_campaign_bundle_path_contract`).
  2. `rch exec -- cargo check -p fsqlite-e2e --all-targets` => exit 0.
- No code changes from BlackDove here; this is verification/unblock work on top of the existing tree state so downstream Track A/G beads can trust the matrix contract.","created_at":"2026-03-10T18:28:27Z"}]}
